name: PM Kisan Samman Nidhi
description: Pradhan Mantri Kisan Samman Nidhi (PM-KISAN) is a Central Sector scheme with 100% funding from Government of India. It aims to provide income support to all landholding farmer families across the country to enable them to take care of expenses related to agriculture and allied activities as well as domestic needs.
eligibility:
1. All landholding farmer families are eligible.
2. Exclusions: Institutional landholders, families with one or more members as serving or retired officers/employees of Government, taxpayers, professionals (doctors, lawyers, etc.), and retired pensioners with monthly pension of ₹10,000 or more.

name: Karshaka Pension (Kerala)
description: The Kerala Agriculture Workers Pension Scheme provides monthly financial assistance to elderly agricultural workers. It is one of the oldest welfare schemes in Kerala, ensuring social security for those who spent their lives in soil cultivation.
eligibility:
1. Must be a permanent resident of Kerala.
2. Age must be 60 years or above.
3. Must have worked in agriculture for at least 10 years.
4. Must be a member of the Agricultural Workers' Welfare Fund Board.
5. Annual family income must be below ₹1 Lakh.

name: Post-Matric Scholarship (Central)
description: Central scholarship for students from reserved categories to ensure no student is deprived of higher education due to financial constraints. It covers tuition, academic fees, and a fixed maintenance allowance.
eligibility:
1. Indian Nationals who are students from SC/ST/OBC/Minority categories.
2. Pursuing studies in recognized schools/colleges (Class 11 to PhD).
3. Family income must be under ₹2.5 Lakh per annum for SC/ST and ₹1.5-2 Lakh for OBC/Minorities.

name: E-Grantz 3.0 (Kerala)
description: E-Grantz is Kerala Government's flagship portal for educational assistance. It handles all scholarships for SC, ST, OBC, and OEC (Other Eligible Communities) students studying in Kerala, from Plus Two onwards.
eligibility:
1. Students must be residents of Kerala.
2. Studying in government or aided colleges/universities.
3. Attendance must be at least 75% for release of monthly stipends.
4. Income thresholds apply for General and OBC categories.

name: Startup India Seed Fund
description: Providing financial assistance to startups for proof of concept, prototype development, product trials, market entry, and commercialization. The scheme supports innovative ideas that can solve real-world problems.
eligibility:
1. Startup recognized by DPIIT, incorporated not more than 2 years ago.
2. Must have a viable business idea and use of technology in product/services.
3. Not have received more than ₹10 Lakh in monetary support from other Central/State schemes.

name: K-DISC OLOI (Kerala)
description: One Local Government One Idea (OLOI). This initiative by K-DISC encourages local bodies to promote innovative enterprises. It provides mentoring and financial links for local innovators to scale their businesses.
eligibility:
1. Aspiring entrepreneurs, SHGs, or Micro-enterprises in Kerala.
2. Idea must address a local need or improve a local process.
3. Must be willing to operate within the local self-government area.

name: PM Mudra Yojana
description: Providing loans up to ₹10 Lakh to non-corporate, non-farm small/micro-enterprises. These loans are classified as Shishu, Kishore, and Tarun based on the stage of growth and funding needs of the beneficiary micro-unit.
eligibility:
1. Any Indian citizen who is eligible to borrow and has a business plan for non-farm income generating activity.
2. Applicable for small manufacturing units, service sector units, artisans, etc.
documents: Project Report for the proposed business

name: Subhiksha Keralam Subsidies
description: A comprehensive food security project by the Kerala Government, providing subsidies for fallow land cultivation, vegetable farming, poultry, and fish farming to achieve self-reliance in food production.
eligibility:
1. Farmers, SHGs, and Kudumbashree units in Kerala.
2. Must register on the AIMS (Agriculture Information Management System) portal.
documents: Location Map of the farm site

name: Kudumbashree Start-up Village Entrepreneurship
description: Aims to support Kudumbashree members to set up micro-enterprises in rural areas. Provides handholding, training, and financial support (CEF - Community Enterprise Fund).
eligibility:
1. Must be a member of Kudumbashree NHG in Kerala.
2. Resident of a rural block covered by the SVEP programme.
